key_insight: The paper shows that zero-shot dynamical-systems reconstruction can be achieved by a minimal, interpretable two-parameter model that blends the current latent state with the nearest context neighbor and its successor.
engineering_relevance: This suggests foundation-model-style forecasting for dynamical systems may be made far smaller, cheaper, and more transparent while still preserving strong out-of-domain performance.
method: Iteratively reduce the SOTA DynaMix architecture to a minimal form called DynaBase.
Use a linear blend of the current latent state, nearest in-context neighbor, and that neighbor’s temporal successor.
Derive closed-form one-step prediction solutions and optimize directly on reconstruction metrics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports competitive zero-shot reconstruction across chaotic and cyclic dynamical systems, with parameter counts orders of magnitude below other foundation models.
watch_item: Despite its simplicity and interpretability, performance may depend on training strategy and can trade off short-term prediction optimality against reconstruction quality.
paper_title: A Minimal Interpretable Architecture for Zero-Shot Reconstruction of Dynamical Systems
